engineIntelligence.h2: Fiat Stilo Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Fiat Stilo in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Fiat Stilo 937A5000 engine - diesel 1.9L replacement UK
knownFailures: Injector seal wear - fuel leaks and rough idle reported from 70k miles on high-mileage units.
Turbocharger failure - oil starvation causes turbo seizure, blue smoke and power loss.
Cylinder head cracks - thermal stress on 8v heads can cause coolant/oil mixing.

cards.altText: Fiat Stilo 182B6000 engine - petrol 1.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing belt failure - interference engine; belt snap causes bent valves requiring full replacement.
ECU IAW5NF faults - erratic throttle, stalling and no-start issues common on early units.
Head gasket seepage - coolant loss and overheating reported on 90k+ mile examples.

cards.altText: Fiat Stilo 192A1000 engine - petrol 2.4L replacement UK
knownFailures: Crankshaft bearing failure - documented bottom-end knock on Abarth units, sometimes below 60k miles.
Valve stem seal wear - oil consumption increases on high-mileage five-cylinder units.
Selespeed hydraulic pump strain - automated manual adds load to engine lubrication system.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,800-£3,500 (supply only) Estimated from comparable 2.4 20v units - actual quotes may vary.

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: The workhorse 1.9 JTD 8v common-rail diesel, powering most UK Stilo diesels with strong torque and decent economy.
compatibleVariants: 1.9 JTD 80, 1.9 JTD 115, 1.9 JTD 120, Multiwagon 1.9 JTD 115, Multiwagon 1.9 JTD 120
commonFailures: Injector seal wear causing fuel leaks and rough idle from 70k miles
Turbocharger oil starvation leading to seizure and blue smoke
Cylinder head cracking on high-mileage 8v units under thermal stress
entries.cta: Get quotes for 937A5000 Fiat Stilo engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Later mapping variant of the 1.9 JTD 8v, offering refined performance and emissions compliance.
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging causing power loss and rough running on short-journey cars
DPF-related backpressure issues on post-2005 units with added emissions hardware
entries.cta: Get quotes for 192B5000 Fiat Stilo engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: The 16-valve Multijet evolution of the 1.9 JTD, delivering higher power and smoother operation for late-model Stilo.
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump wear causing hard starting and power loss
Swirl flap actuator failure on inlet manifold affecting emissions and response

entries.history: The most common UK Stilo engine, a reliable 1.6 16v FIRE unit powering Active and Dynamic trims throughout the model's life.

IAW5NF ECU faults leading to erratic throttle, stalling and no-start conditions
Head gasket seepage on high-mileage examples causing coolant loss

entries.history: Updated 1.6 16v variant for facelift Stilo models, featuring refined mapping and emissions compliance.
commonFailures: Variable valve timing solenoid wear causing rough idle and reduced power
Oil leak from rocker cover gasket and sump seal on higher-mileage units

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: The 1.8 16v unit offered in Dynamic and Sporting trims, delivering stronger performance but with known bottom-end concerns.

Timing chain tensioner wear on early units leading to rattling and timing drift

commonFailures: Head gasket failure on high-mileage units causing coolant loss and overheating
Radiator and cooling system leaks leading to temperature management issues

commonProblems.h2: Fiat Stilo Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Fiat Stilo's engine range includes robust units like the 1.6 16v FIRE and 1.9 JTD diesel, but also known weak points such as the 1.8 16v and Abarth 2.4 20v crankshafts. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this section to decide whether repair or replacement offers the best value for your Stilo.

problems.h4: Fiat Stilo 1.8 16v Crankshaft Failure - Why 192A4000 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Inadequate crankshaft bearing clearance and oil starvation under hard driving leads to bearing wear, knocking and eventual bottom-end failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Oil pan removal, bearing inspection, oil pump check
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable before knocking starts; does not fix existing damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New crankshaft, bearings, pistons, full gasket set, machining
repairOptions.longevity: Restores bottom end; suitable if cylinder head is sound

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt long block with upgraded bearings, tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for terminal failure; addresses root cause with improved components

problems.recommendation: On a Stilo 1.8 worth £800-£1,800, a full rebuilt engine from £1,100 often represents better value than a short-block rebuild, especially given the known bearing design weakness. We recommend sourcing a unit with upgraded crankshaft bearings.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 192A4000 engine prices for your Fiat Stilo

problems.h4: Fiat Stilo Abarth 2.4 Crankshaft Failure - Five-Cylinder Bottom-End Risk
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Stilo Abarth 2.4 20v Manual, Abarth 2.4 20v Selespeed (2001-2007, 192A1000)
problems.rootCause: High-revving five-cylinder design places exceptional load on crankshaft bearings; oil starvation or manufacturing variance can cause premature bearing failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full oil flush, pressure test, bearing clearance check via borescope
repairOptions.longevity: Only preventative; cannot repair existing bearing damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt five-cylinder long block with upgraded bearings, balanced, tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable solution for confirmed crank failure; specialist rebuild essential

problems.recommendation: Given the Abarth Stilo's enthusiast value (£2,000-£4,500), investing in a specialist rebuilt engine with upgraded bearings is the only reliable path after crankshaft failure. Avoid cheap used replacements without documented bearing inspection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 192A1000 Abarth engine prices for your Fiat Stilo

problems.group: Fiat Stilo 1.9 JTD Injector & Turbo Failure
problems.h4: Fiat Stilo 1.9 JTD Injector & Turbo Failure - Diesel High-Mileage Wear
problems.rootCause: High-pressure injector seals degrade allowing fuel dilution of oil; turbocharger oil starvation from clogged feed lines causes bearing wear and seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injectors, replace seals and copper washers, reset adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if turbo is healthy and no cylinder damage; addresses fuel leak only

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and replace turbocharger, clean oil feed, replace gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if engine block is sound; does not address injector wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt diesel long block with tested injectors and turbo, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage units with multiple failures; addresses root causes

problems.recommendation: For Stilo 1.9 JTD models valued at £600-£1,500, a rebuilt engine from £1,500 often makes more sense than sequential injector and turbo repairs, especially if the car has exceeded 100k miles.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Stilo 1.9 JTD rebuilt engines

problems.h4: Fiat Stilo Timing Belt Failure - Interference Engine Catastrophe
problems.affectedModels: All Fiat Stilo petrol engines (1.2 16v, 1.4 16v, 1.6 16v, 1.8 16v, 2.4 20v) - interference design
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 40,000 - 72,000 miles if belt not replaced per schedule
problems.rootCause: Timing belt snaps due to age, oil contamination or missed service; pistons strike open valves causing bent valves, damaged pistons and cylinder head damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idlers, water pump; reset service interval
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable BEFORE belt failure; essential maintenance every 36k miles or 3 years

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt long block with new valves, guides, gaskets; tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only solution after belt snap; addresses bent valves and potential head damage

problems.recommendation: There is no economical repair after timing belt failure on Stilo petrol engines. If your belt is overdue or you hear rattling from the timing cover, replace the belt kit immediately. After failure, a rebuilt engine from £995 is typically better value than attempting valve repair.
problems.cta: Compare Fiat Stilo petrol engine replacement prices

problems.group: Fiat Stilo ECU Failure Misdiagnosed as Engine Fault
problems.h4: Fiat Stilo ECU Failure Misdiagnosed as Engine Fault
problems.rootCause: ECU solder joints crack from thermal cycling; moisture ingress causes intermittent throttle, stalling or no-start that owners mistake for mechanical engine failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove ECU, repair solder joints, test on bench, reprogram if needed
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective if engine is mechanically sound; resolves electrical fault only

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine + ECU Check
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt long block plus verified ECU; eliminates uncertainty about root cause
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if engine has mechanical wear AND ECU faults; comprehensive solution

problems.recommendation: Before condemning the engine, have the ECU professionally tested - many "engine failure" Stilos simply need an ECU repair from £200. If the engine also has high mileage or mechanical symptoms, a rebuilt unit with verified ECU offers peace of mind.
problems.cta: Get a diagnostic check for your Fiat Stilo ECU before engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Fiat Stilo
finalCta.paragraph: Whether your Stilo has suffered timing belt failure, crankshaft wear, or injector issues, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Fiat Stilo specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Fiat Stilo Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Fiat Stilo and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Fiat Stilo Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Fiat Stilo is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Fiat Stilo 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.9L JTD common-rail diesel powered most UK Stilo diesel variants, offering strong low-end torque and respectable economy. Available in 8-valve (80-120hp) and later 16-valve Multijet (140-150hp) forms, it remains the most sought-after diesel replacement engine for Stilo owners.
commonFailurePoints: cylinder head cracks on high-mileage 8v units
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Stilo 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Fiat Stilo 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level 1.2L 16v FIRE engine appeared on early Stilo Active models, offering economical running but lower performance. Less common in replacement demand but still relevant for budget-conscious owners of early 2001-2003 examples.
items.cta: Compare Fiat Stilo 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Fiat Stilo 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: Introduced on facelift Stilo models, the 1.4L 16v FIRE engine provided a balance of economy and adequate performance for urban and motorway driving. A practical choice for later Active and Dynamic trims.
items.cta: Compare Fiat Stilo 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Fiat Stilo 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The most common UK Stilo engine, the 1.6L 16v FIRE unit powered Active and Dynamic trims throughout the model's life. Reliable when maintained, but vulnerable to timing belt failure and ECU issues if neglected.
items.cta: Compare Fiat Stilo 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Fiat Stilo 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: Offered in Dynamic and Sporting trims, the 1.8L 16v delivered stronger performance but carries a known risk of crankshaft bearing wear. Replacement engines should feature upgraded bearings to address this weakness.
commonFailurePoints: timing chain tensioner wear on early units
items.cta: Compare Fiat Stilo 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Fiat Stilo 2.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The distinctive five-cylinder 2.4L 20v Abarth engine delivered 170hp and a unique exhaust note for enthusiast owners. Known for crankshaft bearing failure on high-revving examples; specialist rebuilds essential.
items.cta: Compare Fiat Stilo 2.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Fiat Stilo Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.9L JTD common-rail diesel dominated Stilo diesel sales, offering strong torque and economy but with known injector and turbo wear on high-mileage examples.
items.descriptor: The 1.9L JTD common-rail diesel dominated Stilo diesel sales, offering strong torque and economy but with known injector and turbo wear on high-mileage examples.

families: 937A5000 - 1.9 JTD 8v 80-120hp, most common diesel replacement engine
192B5000 - 1.9 JTD 8v 100-115hp, later mapping variant with refined emissions
937A2000 - 1.9 Multijet 16v 140-150hp, rare high-output diesel for late models
knownFor: Strong low-end torque ideal for motorway cruising
Injector seal wear causing fuel leaks from 70k miles
Turbocharger oil starvation on neglected examples
High UK replacement demand on Multiwagon estate variants

items.title: Fiat Stilo Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Stilos used Fiat's FIRE engine family from 1.2L to 2.4L, with the 1.6 16v being most common and the Abarth 2.4 20v offering enthusiast performance.
items.descriptor: Petrol Stilos used Fiat's FIRE engine family from 1.2L to 2.4L, with the 1.6 16v being most common and the Abarth 2.4 20v offering enthusiast performance.

families: 182B6000 - 1.6 16v FIRE 103hp, most common UK Stilo petrol engine
192A1000 - 2.4 20v Abarth 170hp, five-cylinder with crank failure risk
knownFor: Interference design: timing belt failure = bent valves
IAW5NF ECU faults on 1.6 16v causing stalling and no-start

engineYears.h2: Fiat Stilo Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Fiat Stilo's UK production timeline (2001-2007), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Stilo launched with core FIRE petrol engines and the new 1.9 JTD diesel, establishing the model's engine lineup.
keyChanges: Fiat Stilo hatchback launched in UK with 1.2, 1.6, 1.8 and 2.4 Abarth petrol engines
1.9 JTD 8v diesel introduced with common-rail injection
Early ECU variants (IAW5NF) prone to solder joint issues
knownFor: Launch-year electrical gremlins and ECU faults
Timing belt critical on all petrol interference engines
Abarth crankshaft bearing concerns emerge in enthusiast forums
years.cta: Get quotes for 2001-2003 Fiat Stilo replacement engines
years.ticker: Fiat Stilo 1.6 16v engine replacement • Fiat Stilo Abarth crankshaft failure • Fiat Stilo 1.9 JTD injector leak

years.description: The facelifted Stilo introduced updated 1.4/1.6 engines and the 16-valve Multijet diesel, refining the lineup for later production.
keyChanges: 1.4 16v FIRE engine added for entry-level trims
Updated 1.6 16v (192B6000) with 105hp mapping for late models
1.9 Multijet 16v diesel (140-150hp) introduced on high-spec variants
Multiwagon estate variant gains wider engine availability
knownFor: Improved emissions compliance on facelift engines
Multijet diesel offers stronger performance but higher replacement cost
ECU faults persist on 1.6 16v; timing belt remains critical maintenance item
years.cta: Get quotes for 2004-2007 Fiat Stilo replacement engines
years.ticker: Fiat Stilo 1.6 105bhp engine • Fiat Stilo Multijet 16v replacement • Fiat Stilo Multiwagon diesel engine

years.description: The Stilo Multiwagon estate extended the model's life with practical diesel and petrol options for family buyers.
keyChanges: Multiwagon estate body style launched with dedicated trim levels
1.6 16v and 1.9 JTD engines adapted for estate weight and load
Later Multiwagons received facelift engines and Multijet diesel option
knownFor: Estate variants command slightly higher replacement engine prices due to demand
1.9 JTD remains the preferred Multiwagon engine for towing and load carrying
Same timing belt and ECU concerns apply as hatchback variants
years.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Stilo Multiwagon replacement engines

faq.h2: Fiat Stilo Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Fiat Stilo engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Fiat Stilo engine replacement costs range from £495 for a used 1.2 16v unit to £3,500 for a rebuilt Abarth 2.4 20v engine, with the most common 1.6 16v rebuilt engines priced £995-£1,400 supply only.
keyPoints: Used 1.6 16v engines start around £495-£700 supply only
Rebuilt 1.9 JTD diesel engines typically £1,500-£2,200
Abarth 2.4 20v rebuilt units command £1,800-£3,500 due to rarity
Supply & fit labour adds £350-£850 depending on engine complexity
items.cta: Compare Fiat Stilo engine replacement prices now

items.question: What engines fit a Fiat Stilo and are they interchangeable?
items.answer: Fiat Stilo engines are model-specific; while some codes like 182B6000 1.6 16v were shared with Punto/Marea, ECU mappings and mountings differ, requiring exact code matching.
keyPoints: Always match engine code (e.g., 182B6000 vs 192B6000) not just displacement
1.9 JTD engines share codes with Alfa 147/156 but ECU calibration varies
Abarth 2.4 20v is unique to Stilo Abarth; no direct interchange
DVLA registration lookup ensures exact compatibility before purchase
items.cta: Get a compatibility check for your Fiat Stilo engine code

items.question: How long does a Fiat Stilo engine typically last?
items.answer: Well-maintained Fiat Stilo engines can reach 120,000-150,000 miles; the 1.6 16v FIRE and 1.9 JTD are most durable, while 1.8 16v and Abarth 2.4 may suffer crankshaft issues earlier.
keyPoints: 1.6 16v FIRE engines often exceed 120k miles with timely belt changes
1.9 JTD diesels can reach 150k miles with injector and turbo care
1.8 16v and Abarth 2.4 may experience crank wear from 60k-80k miles
Full service history and correct oil intervals significantly extend life
items.cta: Find a rebuilt Fiat Stilo engine with upgraded components

items.question: What warranty comes with a Fiat Stilo replacement engine?
items.answer: Used Fiat Stilo engines typically carry 30-90 day warranties, while rebuilt and reconditioned units from EngineMarket specialists include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.
keyPoints: Used engines: 30-90 day warranty common, check terms carefully
Reconditioned units: 3-6 month warranty typical from reputable suppliers
Rebuilt engines via EngineMarket: minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Always confirm warranty covers both parts and labour for fitment
items.cta: Compare Fiat Stilo engine warranty terms from UK specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Fiat Stilo?
items.answer: With Stilo values typically £500-£2,500, engine replacement makes sense if the body, interior and electrics are sound; rebuilt engines from £995 can extend life cost-effectively.
keyPoints: Consider total vehicle condition: rust, trim, electronics before investing
Rebuilt 1.6 16v from £995 often cheaper than major repairs on other components
Abarth 2.4 enthusiasts may justify £1,800+ rebuild for a well-kept example
Use EngineMarket comparison to ensure you're paying a fair market price
items.cta: Get quotes to decide if Fiat Stilo engine replacement is worthwhile

items.question: Where can I buy a reliable Fiat Stilo engine in the UK?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK Fiat Stilo engine specialists offering used, reconditioned and rebuilt units with warranty, avoiding the risk of unknown-history breakers.
keyPoints: Avoid unknown-history breakers; request compression test reports
EngineMarket specialists provide warranty-backed rebuilt options
Abarth 2.4 engines require specialist suppliers due to rarity
Registration lookup ensures you receive the exact compatible engine code
items.cta: Find trusted Fiat Stilo engine suppliers via EngineMarket

items.question: Where is the engine number located on a Fiat Stilo?
items.answer: The Fiat Stilo engine code is stamped on the block near the gearbox flange; on 1.2/1.4 engines it may be on the exhaust side, often requiring a mirror to read.
keyPoints: Check block near gearbox mounting flange first
Clean area with degreaser; use mirror and torch for visibility
Code format: e.g., 182B6000, 937A5000 (not the VIN)
Photograph the stamp for verification before ordering replacement
items.cta: Verify your Fiat Stilo engine code before getting quotes

items.question: How quickly can a Fiat Stilo engine be delivered in the UK?
items.answer: Most Fiat Stilo engines in UK stock can be delivered next working day via pallet courier (£50-£80 mainland), with supply & fit options available through local specialist garages.
keyPoints: Common engines (1.6 16v, 1.9 JTD) often in stock for next-day dispatch
Rare units (Abarth 2.4, Multijet 16v) may require 3-5 day sourcing
Pallet delivery to UK mainland typically £50-£80; confirm before ordering
Supply & fit specialists can often fit within 1-2 days of engine arrival
items.cta: Check live availability for your Fiat Stilo engine code

items.question: Can I get supply and fit for a Fiat Stilo engine near me?
items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket's network includes specialist Italian car garages across the UK offering supply & fit for Fiat Stilo engines, with labour from £350 for 1.6 16v to £850 for Abarth 2.4.
keyPoints: Labour costs vary by engine complexity and garage location
Supply & fit includes ancillaries, fluids and ECU coding where needed
Specialist garages familiar with Stilo quirks reduce fitment risks
Request itemised quote separating engine cost and labour for transparency
items.cta: Find Fiat Stilo engine supply & fit deals near you

items.question: Should I choose a rebuilt or used Fiat Stilo engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Fiat Stilo engines offer upgraded components addressing known failures and 12-month warranty, while used engines are cheaper but carry unknown history; choose based on budget and intended ownership duration.
keyPoints: Rebuilt: addresses crank bearings on 1.8/Abarth, new seals on 1.9 JTD
Used: lower upfront cost but verify compression and service history
For cars worth £1,500+, rebuilt often better long-term value
EngineMarket comparison shows both options side-by-side for your code
